$MSFT, $META and $AMZN are ridiculously undervalued. 
 
Michael Mauboussin says 65% of the S&P 500 market cap has historically been from steady-state value, while 35% is from future growth. 
 
I l
posts.body:
$MSFT, $META and $AMZN are ridiculously undervalued. 
 
Michael Mauboussin says 65% of the S&P 500 market cap has historically been from steady-state value, while 35% is from future growth. 
 
I looked at how this is for $MSFT, $AMZN, and $META by capitalising their NTM earnings using the cost of capital. 
 
Currently, 55% of the S&P 500 market cap is basically made up by the present value of the future growth opportunities (PVGO), which is the inverse of the historical averages. 
 
Mean
posts.author: MarketMaestro1

posts.title:
$MSFT, $META, and $AMZN 
 
There’s a clear disconnect between quality and how the market is pricing growth. 
 
Using a framework similar to Mauboussin’s steady-state vs growth breakdown, a large porti
posts.body:
$MSFT, $META, and $AMZN 
 
There’s a clear disconnect between quality and how the market is pricing growth. 
 
Using a framework similar to Mauboussin’s steady-state vs growth breakdown, a large portion of S&P 500 value today is being driven by PVGO rather than steady-state earnings — well above historical norms. 
 
When I run a simple NTM earnings capitalization using cost of capital assumptions, these mega-caps still show a heavier reliance on steady-state value than the index average.

posts.body:
$SPY $QQQ $BTC.X $MU $SNDK 

My thesis is that the U.S. has entered an asset-inflation regime driven by fiscal deficits, liquidity expansion, passive equity flows, and policy dependence on rising asset prices. 

Inflation is being transmitted less through wages and goods, and more through financial assets, producing a K-shaped economy where asset owners compound wealth while non-asset owners fall behind.

Spx 8250+ by October

posts.body:
Other areas of reform

The task forces will take aim at a broad swath of Fed operations.

On the balance sheet, Warsh has long objected to the Fed’s large position in bond markets, which swelled during and after the financial crisis of 2008, as well as in the Covid pandemic in 2020.

There also will be a study of how the Fed gauges inflation after being above its goal for five years following the erroneous “transitory” call in 2021 and 2022. Artificial intelligence and its impacts also will be i

posts.body:
Last July, Warsh, in a CNBC interview while he was campaigning for the job, called for “regime change” at the Fed and cited a “credibility deficit” caused by “incumbents” at the institution. In its place were comments about how “incredibly impressed” he was with what he’d seen in his first weeks on the job and how the meeting “exemplified the very best of the Fed’s traditions.”

What once looked like a potentially rancorous atmosphere inside the institution quickly become collegial as Warsh look

The Fed held rates steady but spoiled the mood with a much more hawkish dot plot. Elevated inflation makes that understandable, but the committee is far from united, with only about half still penciling in rate hikes later this year,” said Sonu Varghese, chief macro strategist at Carson Group. “The bigger point is that policy still looks loose for an economy where inflation remains a problem and the labor market is stabilizing.”

$MSFT, $META and $AMZN are ridiculously undervalued. 
 
Michael Mauboussin says 65% of the S&P 500 market cap has historically been from steady-state value, while 35% is from future growth. 
 
I l
posts.body:
$MSFT, $META and $AMZN are ridiculously undervalued. 
 
Michael Mauboussin says 65% of the S&P 500 market cap has historically been from steady-state value, while 35% is from future growth. 
 
I looked at how this is for $MSFT, $AMZN, and $META by capitalising their NTM earnings using the cost of capital. 
 
Currently, 55% of the S&P 500 market cap is basically made up by the present value of the future growth opportunities (PVGO), which is the inverse of the historical averages. 
 
Mean
posts.author: MarketMaestro1

posts.title:
$MSFT, $META, and $AMZN 
 
There’s a clear disconnect between quality and how the market is pricing growth. 
 
Using a framework similar to Mauboussin’s steady-state vs growth breakdown, a large porti
posts.body:
$MSFT, $META, and $AMZN 
 
There’s a clear disconnect between quality and how the market is pricing growth. 
 
Using a framework similar to Mauboussin’s steady-state vs growth breakdown, a large portion of S&P 500 value today is being driven by PVGO rather than steady-state earnings — well above historical norms. 
 
When I run a simple NTM earnings capitalization using cost of capital assumptions, these mega-caps still show a heavier reliance on steady-state value than the index average.

MU.name: Micron Technology
MU.blurb: The community is bullish on MU due to AI memory demand, but some warn of a post-earnings drop.

SPY.name: SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust
SPY.blurb: SPY has mixed sentiment, with traders watching for a market pullback after recent gains.

MSFT.name: Microsoft
MSFT.blurb: MSFT is seen as weak and underperforming, with bearish sentiment dominating.

NVDA.name: NVIDIA
NVDA.blurb: NVDA is extremely bullish, seen as the core AI play, though new investors are panicking.

JD.name: Jingdong Mall
JD.blurb: JD is very bullish, driven by European expansion and strong promotions, but technicals are shaky.

SNDK.name: Sandisk
SNDK.blurb: SNDK is mentioned alongside MU, with focus on inflation data and storage demand.

SPCX.name: SpaceX
SPCX.blurb: SPCX is bullish, with talk of a merger with TSLA and geopolitical tensions boosting interest.

QQQ.name: Invesco QQQ ETF
QQQ.blurb: QQQ is bearish, with traders expecting tech to lag the broader market.

ESG.name: FlexShares Trust - FlexShares STOXX US ESG Impact Index Fund
ESG.blurb: ESG has a small but bullish following, discussing sustainability initiatives and index inclusions.

AMD.name: AMD
AMD.blurb: AMD is bullish, seen as a beneficiary of AI, but with less hype than NVDA.

VOO.name: Vanguard S&P 500 ETF
VOO.blurb: VOO is mentioned as a core holding, with neutral to bullish sentiment.

META.name: Meta Platforms (Facebook)
META.blurb: META is bearish, with some expecting a parabolic move but others warning of a slaughter.

MLTX.blurb: MLTX is extremely bullish ahead of Phase 3 data, with high expectations for its depression drug.

DFTX.blurb: DFTX is extremely bullish, awaiting topline results for its depression treatment.

BTC.X.blurb: BTC.X has mixed sentiment, with bullish divergence noted but also warnings of a collapse.

sentiment.reasoning: The overall mood is cautiously bullish, driven by strong positive sentiment on key tech names like NVDA and JD, and a surge in mentions for MU. However, mixed signals from SPY and QQQ, along with bearish chatter on MSFT and META, keep confidence moderate.

themes.title: AI and Semiconductor Surge
themes.explanation: MU, NVDA, AMD, and MRVL are heavily discussed, with MU jumping from 139 to 195 mentions. The community is bullish on memory and AI chips, expecting continued demand. Skeptics worry about overvaluation, but the crowd sees earnings growth.

themes.title: Index ETF Divergence
themes.explanation: SPY and QQQ are both in the top 10, but sentiment differs: SPY has a neutral tilt (17 bullish vs 14 bearish), while QQQ is bearish (10 bullish vs 20 bearish). This suggests traders expect tech to underperform the broader market near-term.

themes.title: China Tech Resurgence
themes.explanation: JD saw a massive jump in mentions (21 to 58) and extremely bullish sentiment (21 bullish vs 3 bearish). The community is excited about JD's Joybuy Europe expansion and 618 promotion, betting on a turnaround.

themes.title: Space and Speculation
themes.explanation: SPCX (SpaceX) remains a hot topic despite dropping in rank, with 45 mentions and a bullish tilt (27 vs 13). Posts discuss a potential merger with TSLA and geopolitical tensions, reflecting high retail interest in speculative plays.

themes.title: ESG and Thematic Investing
themes.explanation: ESG jumped from 0 to 38 mentions, with 8 bullish and 0 bearish tags. The community is discussing TELUS's biodiversity strategy and Wesco's inclusion in Dow Jones indices, indicating a niche but growing interest in sustainable investing.

themes.title: Biotech Catalysts
themes.explanation: MLTX and DFTX appear in Stocktwits sentiment with extremely bullish tags (43 and 32 bullish, 0 bearish). MLTX is awaiting Phase 3 data for its depression drug, while DFTX has topline results coming. High risk, high reward.

tickers_to_watch.reason: MU is the most mentioned stock on Reddit, with mentions up 40% in 24 hours. Stocktwits sentiment is bullish (11 vs 7). The community is focused on its earnings and AI memory demand, but some warn of a pullback after recent gains.

NVDA has strong bullish sentiment on Stocktwits (18 vs 2) and mentions surged from 33 to 64. The crowd sees it as a core AI play, though some posts joke about new investors panicking. The bullish bias is clear.

JD's mentions tripled and Stocktwits sentiment is overwhelmingly bullish (21 vs 3). The community is hyped about its European expansion and record promotions. However, the stock is testing technical support, so a breakout could be significant.

QQQ has the most bearish sentiment among major tickers (10 bullish vs 20 bearish). Despite rising mentions, the crowd expects tech to lag. This contrarian view suggests caution for tech-heavy portfolios.

MLTX has an extremely bullish Stocktwits sentiment (43 vs 0). The community is awaiting Phase 3 data for its depression drug, with high expectations. This is a high-risk biotech play with binary outcomes.

risk_factors.title: Tech Overconcentration
risk_factors.explanation: The top mentions are heavily tech-focused, with MU, NVDA, MSFT, and AMD dominating. If the sector faces a correction, these stocks could drop sharply. The bearish sentiment on QQQ suggests some traders are already hedging.

risk_factors.title: Geopolitical Tensions
risk_factors.explanation: Posts about SPCX and DJT mention the Strait of Hormuz and Iran talks. Any escalation could disrupt markets, especially oil prices. The community is aware but may be underestimating the risk.

risk_factors.title: Biotech Binary Events
risk_factors.explanation: MLTX and DFTX have upcoming data releases that could cause massive swings. While sentiment is bullish, a negative result would lead to sharp losses. Retail investors may be overexposed to these speculative bets.

contrarian_views.title: MSFT and META Are Oversold
contrarian_views.explanation: Despite bearish sentiment on MSFT (7 vs 10) and META (11 vs 13), some argue these mega-caps are oversold and due for a rotation. The crowd is too focused on AI darlings, ignoring the stability and cash flows of these giants.

contrarian_views.title: QQQ Bearishness Is Overdone
contrarian_views.explanation: QQQ has the most bearish tags, but some traders note that the index often bounces after such extreme sentiment. The 'massive green candle' posts suggest a contrarian bullish view, expecting a reversal.

contrarian_views.title: ESG Is a Fading Trend
contrarian_views.explanation: While ESG mentions spiked, the volume is low and the bullish sentiment may be from a small group. Critics argue that ESG investing is losing momentum due to political backlash and underperformance, making this a potential trap.

discussion_digest: The biggest story today is the surge in Micron Technology (MU) mentions, jumping from 139 to 195 in 24 hours, making it the top ticker on Reddit. The community is buzzing about its earnings and AI memory demand, but some seasoned traders are warning of a pullback after a 15% run. On Stocktwits, MU sentiment is bullish (11 vs 7), but the most-liked post sarcastically notes that MU 'always drop on good earning,' hinting at skepticism. Meanwhile, NVIDIA (NVDA) also saw a doubling of mentions to 64, with extremely bullish sentiment (18 vs 2), as retail investors pile into AI plays.

Other notable discussions include JD.com (JD), which tripled its mentions to 58 and has overwhelmingly bullish sentiment (21 vs 3). The community is excited about its Joybuy Europe expansion and record 618 promotion, but technical analysis shows it's testing a key support level. On the bearish side, the Invesco QQQ ETF (QQQ) has the most bearish sentiment (10 vs 20), with traders expecting tech to underperform. The most-liked QQQ post jokes about a 'massive green candle' for the 89th ceasefire, reflecting frustration with market volatility.

There's a clear tension between bulls and bears on mega-cap tech. Microsoft (MSFT) and Meta (META) have bearish sentiment, with posts calling them 'disgusting' and warning of a slaughter. Yet some contrarians argue these stocks are oversold and due for a rotation. Meanwhile, speculative biotechs like MLTX and DFTX have extremely bullish sentiment (43 and 32 bullish, 0 bearish) ahead of key data releases, but the binary nature of these events means high risk. Overall, the crowd is divided: excited about AI and China tech, but wary of a broader tech correction.

overlooked_insights.title: DRAM supply cycle structure
overlooked_insights.insight: Users highlight that memory demand is embedded in every AI workload with a structurally constrained supply cycle, implying pricing power for DRAM and NAND. This is more granular than the generic 'AI memory boom' headlines and suggests a fundamental supply-demand imbalance that could sustain margins.
overlooked_insights.evidence: User message: '$DRAM - memory demand embedded in every AI workload; structurally constrained supply cycle'. Another user groups $MU, $SNDK, $WDC as memory plays.
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream coverage focuses on earnings numbers and headline HBM demand, but lacks analysis of the multi-year supply constraint mechanics that users are discussing.

overlooked_insights.title: Strait of Hormuz closure oil risk
overlooked_insights.evidence: User message: 'Zero ship traffic. Strait is closed. <4 weeks of strategic reserves. If the strait reopened tomorrow, it would be highly unlikely make a difference to stop what is coming; Extreme oil supply shock.'
overlooked_insights.insight: A user claims total closure of the Strait of Hormuz with less than four weeks of U.S. strategic petroleum reserves remaining, implying an imminent oil supply shock that could roil equity markets. Mainstream headlines mention Iran peace deals but downplay the actual shipping disruption.
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Headlines report diplomatic talks and oil price moves but do not quantify the physical closure or reserve depletion timelines discussed on Stocktwits.

overlooked_insights.title: Google's $115B debt in six months
overlooked_insights.evidence: User message: 'How much $GOOG took on debt in 2026? Just 6 months. About 115 billion. Just this year. If they have so much free cash flow, why is it taking on the debt? Absolutely horrendous.'
overlooked_insights.insight: A user claims Alphabet issued $115 billion in debt over the first half of 2026, questioning the logic given its free cash flow. This specific leverage figure is not reported in the mainstream headlines provided, which focus on AI spending and analyst upgrades.
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream outlets discuss AI capex but do not break out the absolute debt issuance number or the apparent inconsistency between cash flow and borrowing.

overlooked_insights.title: Rumble's undervalued data center buy
overlooked_insights.evidence: User messages: 'RUMBLE just acquired 9 DATA CENTERS with 22k $NVDA GPUs and 300k CPUs. Rumble's current market cap is $3 billion. The book value of the those 9 data centers alone is worth $9 billion.'
overlooked_insights.insight: Users point out that Rumble (RUM) bought nine data centers equipped with 22,000 NVIDIA GPUs at a book value of $9 billion, yet its market cap is only $3 billion, implying a deep value discrepancy. This is a specific channel check not covered in the mainstream headlines for NVDA or RUM.
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream coverage of NVDA focuses on CoreWeave and general AI demand, while Rumble is a small-cap that flies under the radar despite this tangible asset acquisition.

overlooked_insights.title: JD.com Joybuy Europe expansion
overlooked_insights.evidence: User message: 'Joybuy Europe in focus' with a link to a cross-border magazine article. Another user links JD's 618 Grand Promotion setting a new record.
overlooked_insights.insight: A user highlights JD.com's Joybuy Europe platform opening to European sellers, which is a specific international expansion initiative. This is not mentioned in the mainstream headlines that focus on regulatory crackdowns and weak consumer demand.
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Headlines concentrate on Alibaba, Beijing's rebuke of discount practices, and the 618 festival, but ignore JD's European push which could be a new growth vector.

overlooked_insights.title: Index inclusion forced buying for SNDK and SPCX
overlooked_insights.evidence: User message: 'June 18: CRSP index inclusion for SpaceX. Triggers an estimated $4-7B in forced buying by passive funds. June 18: FTSE Russell inclusion... $6-9B. June 26: MSCI inclusion' (similar for SNDK).
overlooked_insights.insight: Users identify specific index inclusion dates (CRSP on June 18, FTSE on June 18, MSCI on June 26) with estimated forced buying of $4-9 billion each for Sandisk and SpaceX. These catalyst events are absent from the mainstream headlines, which instead discuss general euphoria and valuation.
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream articles treat SNDK and SPCX as momentum plays without pinpointing the mechanical rebalancing flows that retail traders are front-running.

overlooked_insights.title: Potential NAND supply expansion policy
overlooked_insights.evidence: User message: 'if admin agrees with Tim Cook and Dell and HP to let them compete for low end nand/dram, it just deflates the bubble in $SNDK. All that is needed is we are looking at expanding supply until prices come down, then export controls relax.'
overlooked_insights.insight: One user speculates that the Trump administration could allow Apple, Dell, and HP to enter low-end NAND/DRAM production, flooding supply and deflating SNDK's premium. This regulatory/policy angle is not discussed in the headlines, which focus on Sandisk's AI boom.
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream coverage portrays Sandisk as a pure AI memory play, ignoring the possibility of anti-competitive supply expansion that could reverse the rally.

overlooked_insights.title: MLTX sonelokimab 67% HiSCR75 at 52 weeks
overlooked_insights.evidence: User message: '67% of patients treated with SLK achieved HiSCR75 at the one-year mark, with more than half achieving HiSCR100.' Another user expects 'anything above 50% at Week 16 will be viewed as a massive win' for IZAR-1.
overlooked_insights.insight: Users cite specific efficacy data from the VELA program (67% HiSCR75 at 52 weeks), which is a more granular result than the headlines that only mention 'positive week 52 results'. They also set clear expectations for the upcoming IZAR-1 readout at 16 weeks.
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Headlines summarize the FDA alignment and price target hikes but miss the exact efficacy percentages and the specific benchmark for the next catalyst.

overlooked_insights.title: Fed balance sheet task force reform
overlooked_insights.evidence: User message: 'Big changes brewing at the Fed. Task forces are eyeing the bloated balance sheet from 2008 and 2020, plus a fresh look at how they measure inflation...' Another user references 'Warsh' and 'regime change' at the Fed.
overlooked_insights.insight: Users discuss specific Fed task forces reviewing balance sheet reduction and inflation measurement methodology under new leadership (Warsh). This is not covered in the mainstream headlines, which only mention general Fed policy expectations.
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream financial news focuses on rate decisions and economic data, not the internal task force structure that could signal major policy shifts.

catalysts.event: Investor Day with discussion of IZAR-1 Week 16 results
catalysts.expected_impact: If HiSCR response exceeds 50% at Week 16, shares could see a significant rally; failure would be a major disappointment.

catalysts.event: Webcast discussing topline Phase 3 Emerge study results in Major Depressive Disorder
catalysts.expected_impact: Positive data could drive a sharp move higher; some users are skeptical about the timing and shelf grant news.
Significant passive fund inflows likely to push SNDK higher in the short term.

catalysts.event: CRSP and FTSE Russell index inclusion (combined ~$10-16B forced buying)
catalysts.expected_impact: Massive passive buying could propel SpaceX shares, but may be front-run.

catalysts.event: FTSE Russell index inclusion (~$6-9B forced buying)
catalysts.expected_impact: Additional forced buying pressure beyond CRSP, amplifying upward momentum.
Later wave of passive buying could extend the rally into late June.

catalysts.when: This week (June 24 referenced in one headline)
catalysts.expected_impact: Users are divided; some expect a 'crash' despite good earnings, while others see a catalyst for continued AI memory demand.
Could influence rate cut expectations and move the broad market, especially given the 42% odds of a rate hike that one user noted.

crowd_vs_news.news_narrative: Headlines emphasize the Micron investment thesis based on a single number (likely HBM revenue) and the upcoming earnings as a key event.
crowd_vs_news.crowd_view: Many users are bearish, expecting a pre-market crash and warning that MU 'always drops on good earnings'; some cite geopolitical risk from Israel.
crowd_vs_news.who_is_right: The crowd's skepticism may be warranted given MU's historical pattern of selling off after earnings, but the mainstream thesis could still play out long-term. The geopolitical risk is an unverified overhang.

crowd_vs_news.news_narrative: Mainstream coverage is euphoric, calling Sandisk a 'once-in-a-generation' AI memory play with huge upside, and notes it's up 700% YTD.
crowd_vs_news.crowd_view: Users are aware of the euphoria but also highlight specific index inclusion catalysts and supply expansion risks; some are outright bearish, labeling it a bubble that could deflate if the administration allows competition in low-end NAND.
crowd_vs_news.who_is_right: The crowd's attention to index inclusion mechanics gives them a short-term edge, but the bearish supply policy speculation is just a theory. The mainstream euphoria may have already priced in much of the index buying.

crowd_vs_news.news_narrative: Headlines report strong recent momentum, FDA alignment, and a price target hike, portraying a positive outlook.
crowd_vs_news.crowd_view: Users express nervousness about the data release timing, questioning why results would be discussed rather than released immediately; one asks if the shelf grant is new information.
crowd_vs_news.who_is_right: The crowd's skepticism about data quality is a legitimate concern—if the data were stellar, a press release might suffice. The market's reaction on June 22 will reveal who was correct.

MU.summary: Micron (MU) is getting a lot of chatter on Reddit and Stocktwits, with mentions nearly doubling in the last 24 hours. The stock is ranked #1 on Reddit, indicating high interest. The conversation is mixed but leans bearish. Many traders are frustrated because MU often drops after good earnings, and some are warning that the recent rally might reverse. There's also concern about geopolitical risks, specifically Israel's actions, which could impact markets. On the bullish side, some investors are holding MU for the long term, citing the global expansion of data infrastructure and AI demand. They see the cyclical nature of semiconductors as an opportunity to ride the larger trend. However, the short-term sentiment is cautious, with bears pointing to potential profit-taking and a shaky week ahead. Overall, investors should know that MU is a high-beta stock tied to the memory chip cycle, and while AI demand is a positive catalyst, the stock can be volatile around earnings and geopolitical events.

SPY.summary: SPY, the S&P 500 ETF, is seeing increased discussion on Reddit and Stocktwits, with mentions up from 50 to 66 in 24 hours. The sentiment is mixed but slightly bearish. Some traders are calling the weekly chart 'nasty' and expecting a pullback, while others are bullish on a rally later in the week after MU earnings. There's also frustration with bears overreacting to small dips. Geopolitical concerns, like oil prices and ceasefire talks, are adding uncertainty. Overall, SPY is a broad market proxy, so the discussion reflects general market sentiment. Investors should know that SPY is influenced by macro factors, earnings season, and geopolitical events. The community is divided, with some expecting a short-term dip and others seeing it as a buying opportunity.

MSFT.summary: The online chatter about Microsoft is a mixed bag of frustration and long-term confidence. Many traders are venting about MSFT's price action, complaining that it drops harder than the market on red days but barely rallies when the market is green. There's a sense of exhaustion with the stock's 'disgusting' behavior, as one user put it, feeling like MSFT is a laggard that can't catch a break. Others point out that MSFT is often lumped with other mega-cap tech like META, AMZN, and GOOG as a 'quality' buy, but the short-term sentiment is bearish. Some see it as a value trap, while a few bullish voices predict a parabolic move before earnings. The discussion also touches on data center efficiency and AI infrastructure, with MSFT being part of the 'arms race' alongside Amazon and Google. Overall, the community is split: short-term traders are frustrated, but long-term investors still see it as a consistent performer. Investors should know that MSFT is a battleground stock right now, with bearish sentiment dominating the daily chatter but underlying fundamentals still strong.

NVDA.summary: NVIDIA is generating a lot of buzz, with a clear bullish tilt. The community is excited about AI infrastructure, with mentions of GPU clusters and data center buildouts. A new investor even put their life savings into NVDA, showing strong retail conviction. There's optimism that NVDA will continue to lead the AI theme, with bulls expecting green closes despite red opens. Some users highlight the value in related stocks like Rumble, which acquired data centers with NVDA GPUs, indirectly boosting NVDA's narrative. The sentiment is overwhelmingly positive, with 18 bullish tags vs 2 bearish on Stocktwits. However, there's also a note of caution: one user mentions 'reasons to panic' but focuses on long-term execution. Overall, the community sees NVDA as a core AI play with strong momentum. Investors should know that NVDA is a high-conviction stock among retail traders, driven by AI demand, but volatility is expected.

JD.summary: JD.com is getting a lot of buzz on Reddit and Stocktwits, with mentions more than doubling in the last 24 hours. The stock is currently testing key technical levels, with traders watching the 50-day moving average and trendline support. Many are optimistic about the company's recent 618 Grand Promotion, which set new records for shoppers and showed strong growth in service consumption. There's also excitement around Joybuy's expansion into Europe, which could open new revenue streams. However, some traders are cautious, noting that JD looks weak as it broke below the 0.618 Fibonacci level. The stock is also being lumped with other Chinese ADRs like BABA and BIDU, which have had a disappointing week due to the Hang Seng index tanking. Overall, sentiment is mixed but leaning bullish, with some expecting a 20-30% run before the next earnings report. Investors should watch for stabilization above the 50DMA and any further news on China's stimulus measures, as weak retail data keeps pressure on the government to act.

SNDK.summary: SanDisk (SNDK) is trending on Reddit and Stocktwits, with mentions nearly doubling. The stock is part of the memory sector, which many see as a key beneficiary of AI infrastructure buildout. Traders are comparing SNDK's performance to other memory plays like MU and WDC, noting that SNDK has delivered massive returns (one user claimed $468K from $10K in a year). However, there's debate about whether the rally is sustainable. Some worry that if the US government allows companies like Apple, Dell, and HP to compete in low-end NAND/DRAM, it could deflate the bubble. Others are bullish on memory as a sector for the next decade, citing AI's need for physical infrastructure. The stock is also influenced by macro factors like the upcoming May PCE inflation report, which could impact markets. Overall, sentiment is bullish but cautious, with some bearish voices warning of potential supply expansion. Investors should watch for inflation data and any policy changes regarding memory chip competition.

SPCX.summary: SPCX is getting a lot of chatter, but it's a mixed bag. Some people are bullish because they think the company has huge cash reserves—like $109 billion in a week—and they're comparing it to the Mag 7. There's also talk about it having Bitcoin, Starlink, data centers, and maybe even a merger with Tesla. But others are super bearish, calling it garbage and a falling knife. A lot of the messages are just ticker mentions or complaints about bots. The sentiment seems split: some see it as a long-term hold with Musk's ventures, while others think it's overhyped and risky. The drop in Reddit mentions from 61 to 45 might mean interest is cooling off, but it's still ranked 7th. Investors should know that the bullish case hinges on Musk's empire and massive cash, but the bearish side warns of pie-in-the-sky dreams and potential bankruptcy. The Stocktwits data shows 27 bullish vs 13 bearish tags, so there's more optimism there, but the messages themselves are pretty negative. Overall, it's a high-risk, high-reward play that's heavily tied to Elon Musk's vision.

ESG.summary: The ticker $ESG is not a single company but an ETF or index tracking environmental, social, and governance (ESG) criteria. The recent surge in mentions (38 in 24h vs 0 previously) is driven by a flurry of press releases highlighting corporate sustainability achievements. Messages include TELUS launching a biodiversity strategy, Wesco International joining a Dow Jones sustainability index, ZTE releasing a sustainability report, and Subaru giving away trees. Other mentions include BMO being named one of the world's most ethical companies, and John Doerr's call for clean energy. The sentiment is overwhelmingly positive, with no bearish tags. Investors should know that $ESG reflects a broad basket of companies with strong ESG practices, and the attention is likely due to a coordinated release of sustainability news. However, the ticker's performance depends on the underlying holdings and market sentiment toward ESG investing, which can be influenced by political and regulatory factors. The lack of bearish discussion suggests a bullish tilt, but the volume is still low.

MLTX.summary: MoonLake Immunotherapeutics ($MLTX) is generating a lot of buzz on Stocktwits, with all bullish sentiment and no bearish tags in the recent messages. The excitement centers around the company's Phase 3 VELA program for sonelokimab (SLK) in hidradenitis suppurativa (HS), a chronic inflammatory skin condition. The data released shows that 67% of patients achieved HiSCR75 at one year, which is a high bar for efficacy. Investors are comparing SLK favorably to competitors like Brimzelx, noting that its nanobody structure may allow better joint clearance. There's also anticipation for upcoming IZAR-1 data at 16 weeks, with hopes that response rates above 50% would be a massive win. The community is very optimistic, with some predicting a $25+ move and targeting $50+. However, there is a note of caution from one user who worries about options betting by those who can't afford losses, and another who questions whether the stock will disappoint like $NTLA did on positive data. Overall, the sentiment is overwhelmingly bullish, driven by strong clinical results and the potential for SLK to become a best-in-class therapy. Investors should watch for the investor day on June 22, 2026, and the IZAR-1 readout.

DFTX.summary: Definium Therapeutics ($DFTX) is also seeing a surge in attention on Stocktwits, with all bullish or neutral messages ahead of a major catalyst: topline results from the Phase 3 Emerge study in major depressive disorder (MDD) are expected to be discussed on June 22, 2026. The community is excited, with messages like "tomorrow we ride!" and "big day tomorrow." However, there is some nervousness, as one user questions why results weren't released immediately if they were great, and why a discussion is needed. This suggests some uncertainty about the outcome. The stock is trending alongside other psychedelic or depression-focused biotechs like $CMPS and $ATAI. Investors should note that the company is hosting a live webcast at 8 a.m. EDT to discuss the data. The sentiment is cautiously optimistic, but the lack of bearish tags doesn't mean there aren't risks. The Phase 3 data could be a make-or-break moment for DFTX, and the community is watching closely.

BTC.X.summary: Bitcoin is getting a lot of attention on Stocktwits and Reddit, but the sentiment is mixed. On Stocktwits, there are 16 bullish tags and 20 bearish tags, indicating a slight bearish tilt. The messages show a clear divide: some traders are optimistic about a bullish divergence on the weekly chart, pointing to a potential price increase, while others are extremely bearish, calling Bitcoin a scam and predicting a collapse on Monday. One user sarcastically questions where the billions in trading volume come from if no one is buying, highlighting the debate about demand. Another user promotes crypto as a hedge against inflation, urging people to get into crypto. Overall, the community is polarized, with technical analysis and macro narratives driving the discussion. Investors should be aware that Bitcoin's price is volatile and sentiment can shift quickly. The lack of Reddit mentions suggests limited discussion there, but Stocktwits activity shows active trading chatter. Key factors include the weekly close above March candle, bullish divergence, concerns about wash trading, and the impact of inflation on fiat currencies.
